displayTitle: Half the Job Is Reading the Result

beats.narration: Finding the leak is only half the job. The other half is reading the result: how big, how real, and how dangerous.

payload.title: READ THE RESULT
payload.sub: Where the leak is — and how big it is.

beats.narration: Size is a leak rate. Express it, scale it, and standardize it: no reading means anything until a calibrated leak gives the system its scale.
Now unmask the impostors. A real through-wall leak climbs linearly; a virtual leak from trapped gas rises, then flattens — and helium spray never answers.

payload.heading: Read the rate-of-rise curve
payload.note: FLAT CURVE + NO SPRAY RESPONSE = VIRTUAL LEAK

beats.narration: More impostors, plus the hazard. Permeation soaks through O-rings. Air carries five ppm helium. And compressed gas is stored energy — treat it that way.
So don't just find it — read it. The rate, the impostor, the stored energy. That's where leak testing earns its keep.

payload.sub: Rate, impostor, stored energy — read every one.
